programme.title: Edison
programme.desc: Ambitious young reporter Pollack (Justin Timberlake) suspects insidious corruption in his town's elite police unit, known as F.R.A.T., and finds a friendly source in Deed (LL Cool J), a dissatisfied member of the squad. With Deed's information and the backing of his publisher, Ashford (Morgan Freeman), and a crusading lawyer, Wallace (Kevin Spacey), Pollack infiltrates the secretive police cabal. He finds himself in over his head, however, when he discovers the true extent of the criminality.

programme.title: Labyrinth

programme.title: Sliding Doors
programme.desc: After personal and professional setbacks, a woman experiences an alternate reality.

programme.title: First Shot
programme.desc: A veteran Secret Service agent must thwart an assassin's plan to kill both her and the President.

programme.title: Dirty
programme.desc: Hard-edged story of a gangbanger turned cop who is forced to choose between his conscience and his loyalty to the code of blue all in the course of one day.

programme.title: U-Turn
programme.desc: A small-time gambler gets more than he bargained for when his car breaks down near a remote desert town.

programme.title: Little Nikita
programme.desc: A timely espionage thriller which stars River Phoenix as a seemingly average teenager, except in one respect--his real name is Nikita. River Phoenix has been living a dangerous lie and Sidney Poitier is the secret agent who forces him to face the hard truth about his parents true identity in this riveting spy thriller.

programme.title: Born Yesterday
programme.desc: A million dollar Tycoon hires a tutor to teach his lover proper etiquette.

programme.title: We Were Strangers
programme.desc: An expatriate Cuban revolutionary crafts an ingenious plan to lure the country's corrupt leaders into a deadly trap.

programme.title: Miss Sadie Thompson
programme.desc: A former prostitute inflames the passions of the Marines and a high-minded missionary at a remote South Pacific outpost.

programme.title: Bad For Each Other
programme.desc: An idealistic doctor must choose between the riches from treating a high society clientele or caring for the poor struggling miners in the Pennsylvania coal town where he was born.

programme.title: Love Has Many Faces
programme.desc: Complicated love story of a millionairess married to a beach bum.

programme.title: Fast Vengeance
programme.desc: "Upon hearing of his brother's death, a man named Shen returns to his old stomping grounds to find the men responsible and take justice into his own hands. He learns that a biker named Cobra and his ferocious gang, The Midnight Squad, were the ones responsible ... but to get anywhere close to them, he must enter the dark, turbulent realm of underground motorcycle racing. Enlisting the aid of a drag racing champion-turned-mechanic, Shen must compete in the high-stakes races where he will have one chance to confront Cobra. But when Shen's plan is revealed, he must battle both on the streets and in the ring to obtain his final revenge. Featuring hip-hop legend DMX (Belly, Romeo Must Die), Fast Vengeance is a high-octane action thriller with style — and rubber — to burn."

programme.title: The Loft
programme.desc: Karl Urban and James Marsden star in the tense psychological thriller THE LOFT, the story of five friends who conspire to share a secret penthouse loft in the city - a place where they can indulge their deepest fantasies. But the fantasy becomes a nightmare when they discover the dead body of an unknown woman in the loft and realize one of the group must be involved. Friendships are tested, loyalties are questioned and relationships crumble as the group is consumed by fear, suspicion and murder.

programme.title: Journey to the End of Night
programme.desc: A son and his father separately plot to escape their lives in the underworld of Brazil's sex industry.

programme.title: Filly Brown
programme.desc: FILLY BROWN is an inspiring and gritty portrait of a young artist striving to find her voice and seize her dreams without compromise. Majo Tonorio, aka, "Filly Brown" is a young, raw hip-hop artist who spits rhymes from the heart. With a mother in prison and a father struggling to provide for his daughters, Majo knows that a record contract could be her family's ticket out. But when a record producer offers her a shot at stardom, she is suddenly faced with the prospect of losing who she is as an artist, as well as the friends who helped her reach the cusp of success. Fueled by a fierce hip-hop score, FILLY BROWN is propelled by an exceptional cast featuring Gina Rodriguez, Lou Diamond Phillips, Edward James Olmos and the legendary Jenni Rivera in her final on-screen performance.

programme.title: Hoop Dreams
programme.desc: Two ordinary inner-city kids dare to dream the impossible - â€professional basketball gloryâ€ - in this epic chronicle of hope and faith.

programme.title: Marauders
programme.desc: A group of robbers is chased by an FBI agent who uncovers a deeper conspiracy behind the robbery-homicides.

programme.title: Killshot
programme.desc: An experienced hitman and his unhinged partner target a blue-collar couple who have entered Witness Protection

programme.title: The Way of the Gun
programme.desc: Two criminals get more than they bargained for after kidnapping the surrogate mom of a powerful and shady man.

programme.title: Man on a Ledge
programme.desc: A police psychologist works to talk down an ex-con who is threatening to jump from a Manhattan hotel rooftop.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Enter the Fat Dragon
programme.desc: A police officer investigates the mysterious death of a suspect who was in his custody during a routine transfer.

programme.title: Big Brother

programme.title: Legend of the fist
programme.desc: Chen fakes his death and returns as a caped crime fighter.

programme.desc: A swordsman who is going blind is forced to raise his sword again to save his daughter, who has been kidnapped by a slave dealer.

programme.title: A Deadly Threat to My Family
programme.desc: Ryan's sister escapes from a dangerous cult and seeks refuge with his family.

programme.desc: Author Cheryln Cadle divulges chilling never-before-heard details about Watts.

programme.desc: A young student finds herself the hostage of a deranged criminal.

programme.desc: Ryan's sister escapes from a dangerous cult and seeks refuge with his family.

programme.title: Falling For the Competition
programme.desc: A local mom’s and dad’s group clash for the prestigious Modern Parenting Group of the Year award.

programme.title: Been There All Along
programme.desc: Charley, the founder of a dating app must team up with marketing expert Liam, to save her company.

programme.title: A Match for the Prince
programme.desc: A matchmaker risks falling for a real-life prince before his royal wedding date.

programme.title: Finding Love in Saint Lucia
programme.desc: An Ornithologist and a Journalist partner up to save an endangered bird species in the jungle of Saint Lucia.

programme.title: Call Her King
programme.desc: After sentencing a notorious gangster to death, Judge Jaeda King must rely on her wits, strength and skill when her courtroom is taken over by force by the prisoner's brother.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Chapman is an ex-marine in Brazil's slums, battling the yakuza outfit who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: The New Girl
programme.desc: When her sister Monica suddenly disappears under suspicious circumstances, Malorie becomes convinced that Monica's seemingly perfect husband is hiding something sinister. She then launches her own investigation and unearths a twisted web of lies, abuse, and even political conspiracy. Malorie must confront dangerous secrets that not only threaten her sister's life but her own.

programme.title: Nowhere to be Found
programme.desc: Two sisters go on a senior trip to Mexico, led by Ava’s boyfriend Trent. Meanwhile, journalist Jessica Vandross is going to the same Mexican town in search of her sister’s killer after escaping a sex trafficking ring. These stories collide, in a thrilling fight for their lives.

programme.title: Intrigue
programme.desc: Jessica and Darren's marriage has hit a rocky stage in their lives. When a mysterious man named Michael enters her life, Jessica is intrigued explores options that could have deadly consequences.

programme.title: The Comeback
programme.desc: Jeff and Tisa Murray have decided to move their family back to their hometown of Atlanta when Tisa is offered her dream job. The dream becomes a hilarious reality when family issues, Mom's new boyfriend, and ex-lovers are waiting on them upon their return.

programme.title: The Private Secretary
programme.desc: Pamela and Sterling are the married owners of a thriving tech empire, where they have all the power and money in the world. As the demands of their business have grown, they bring on Vanessa, a high efficient and enigmatic private secretary. What initially seems as a godsend, Vanessa soon begins to dive further into their lives, and has an agenda that could bring their empire to ruin.

programme.title: Threaded Lies
programme.desc: When rising fashion designer Dante prepares to launch his brand, haunting messages and resurfaced betrayals from his former partner threaten to unravel everything. As paranoia sets in, Dante must confront the lies he’s built his success on before they destroy him.

programme.title: Princess of Mars
programme.desc: Based on the Edgar Rice Burroughs novel, a US soldier is transported to Mars in the midst of an alien war.

programme.title: Dragonquest
programme.desc: A young hero must awaken a dragon to battle a legendary creature summoned by a warlord.

programme.title: Disaster Strike Force
programme.desc: Plummeting temperatures signify the dawn of a new Ice Age.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Giant Octopus
programme.desc: The California coast is terrorized by two enormous prehistoric sea creatures.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: After water supplies dry up, the surviving humans have to battle blood-drinking roamers in the apocalypse.

programme.title: Two Evil Eyes
programme.desc: In George Romero's The Facts In The Case Of Mr. Valdemar, a conniving wife and her lover use a hypnotic trance to embezzle a fortune from her dying husband, only to receive some chilling surprises from beyond the grave. Then in Dario Argento's The Black Cat, a deranged crime scene photographer is driven to brutal acts of madness and murder by his girlfriend's new pet.

programme.title: Humanoids From The Deep (Edited Version)
programme.desc: A race of scientifically created half-human, half-salmon monstrosities lay siege to a Pacific Northwest fishing village. In horrific fashion, the mutated creatures kill the men and mate with the women in order to reproduce. Can the Humanoids From the Deep be stopped before they destroy mankind as we know it?Soundtrack scored by Oscar-winning composer James Horner (Avatar, Titanic, Braveheart). Creature design from Oscar-winning make-up artist Rob Bottin (Total Recall, The Thing, Robocop, Se7en).

programme.title: The Ward
programme.desc: In 1966, in North Bend, Oregon, the runaway the police capture Kristen after burning down a farmhouse and is locked in the North Bend Psychiatric Hospital. Kristen is introduced to Dr. Gerald Stringer, who uses experimental therapy. Then she meets the inmates Emily, Sarah, Zoey, and Iris and the tough nurse Lundt. During the night and in the shower later, Kristen sees the ghost of a woman and she learns that she is Alice Leigh Hudson, a mysterious wicked intern that has disappeared. When Iris is ready to go home, she is attacked by the ghost of Alice in the basement and murdered. She vanishes and the inmates decide to seek Iris out. Then Sarah is abducted by Alice and killed; the next one is Emily. Meanwhile, Kristen escapes from her room and meets Zoey, expecting to protect her. However, Alice kidnaps Zoey and Kristen runs to Dr. Stringer's office. She snoops his desk and finds a report with the truth about Alice.

programme.title: Shaky Shivers
programme.desc: From director Sung Kang (star of Fast X), after finding herself bitten by a mysterious animal, Lucy becomes convinced that she will transform into a fearsome werewolf. Joined by her best friend Karen, the two embark on a campy adventure filled with magic and mayhem, as they look to do battle with a throat-slashing creature ripped right out of an 80s horror movie.

programme.title: Two Evil Eyes
programme.desc: In George Romero's The Facts In The Case Of Mr. Valdemar, a conniving wife and her lover use a hypnotic trance to embezzle a fortune from her dying husband, only to receive some chilling surprises from beyond the grave. Then in Dario Argento's The Black Cat, a deranged crime scene photographer is driven to brutal acts of madness and murder by his girlfriend's new pet.

programme.title: The Banishing
programme.desc: A young reverend, his wife and daughter move into a manor with a horrifying secret. When a vengeful spirit haunts the little girl and threatens to tear the family apart, the reverend and his wife are forced to confront their beliefs.

programme.title: The Cellar
programme.desc: About a week after the Woods family move in to Xaos House, their daughter Ellie goes missing during a power cut. The odd thing is, that she was on the phone to her mother Keira at the time, counting down the ten steps into the cellar to find the fuse box. Frustrated by the lack of leads, Keira investigates and finds that the walls have strange symbols engraved into them. After a number of terrifying supernatural experiences, she comes to the conclusion that the house took Ellie. She discovers that a physicist and occultist called John Fetherston, who was obsessed with finding the 11th dimension, built the house and engraved sinister equations on the 10 steps leading into the cellar. Finally, she must battle with the universe's most ancient evil, or lose her family's souls forever.

programme.title: A Dark Song
programme.desc: A heartbroken woman and recluse occultist perform an ancient, dark ritual to bring her child back to life - but missteps have consequences and unknown evils are unleashed.

programme.title: From Black
programme.desc: A recovering drug addict, saddled by crushing guilt after the disappearance of her young son, is presented with a bizarre offer to learn the truth about what happened and set things right.

programme.title: Mandrake
programme.desc: Mandrake follows probation officer Cathy Madden, who is given the task of rehabilitating notorious killer 'Bloody' Mary Laidlaw back into society after twenty years of jail.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Just in time for National Ravioli Day, acclaimed chef and Chopped judge Scott Conant joins the trio at the W South Beach to make his delicate ricotta gnocchiótechnically gnudióalongside his signature Martone Street jarred tomato sauce. Michael has been close friends with Scott for 15 years, and Clinton admits he was genuinely nervous before meeting the notoriously stern Chopped judge, quickly discovering he's warm and funny in person. Scott explains that Martone Street is named for his grandparents' home in Waterbury, Connecticut, where Sunday dinners taught him that conversation over food is one of life's greatest gifts. The gnocchi dough combines fresh ricotta, basil, breadcrumbs, flour, egg, and Parmesan, shaped into small dumplings and cooked until they float. Scott builds a stabilized butter sauce using pasta water and cornstarch as an emulsifieróa trick that keeps it smooth for hours. Finished with Martone Street sauce and broccoli rabe, the result is stunning in its simplicity. His philosophy runs throughout: spaghetti aglio e olio, done correctly, is one of the most complex and satisfying dishes imaginable. Scott becomes emotional talking about watching his 16-year-old daughter drive for the first time and the joy of steak cook-offs with his girls at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Food Network Star winner and Southern food authority Damaris Phillips brings a taste of Louisville poolside at the W South Beach, demonstrating three Southern tea sandwich classics that are anything but fussy. Carla Hall has been singing Damaris's praises for yearsómutual friend Jackie Guido insisted they were destined to be peas and carrotsóand the episode proves her right, the two radiating warmth and joy throughout. Damaris makes Pimento Cheese; Benedictine, a Louisville specialty of grated cucumber with dill and cream cheese (the key being squeezing out all the cucumber water first); and Ham Salad, essentially chicken salad's porky cousin, versatile as either sweet or savory. Everything is layered on Japanese milk bread or thin white bread, crusts removed, and assembled into dainty finger sandwiches and triple-decker stacks. Michael immediately tries to build an enormous open-face sandwich rather than the delicate bites Damaris intended, sparking a lively debate about proportion and restraint. Carla shares that she recently spent a cozy Super Sunday with Damaris in Louisvilleóa beloved tradition of soups, board games, and community. The episode also features a spirited group effort to talk Damaris out of her plan to get a leopard-print face tattoo, with limited success. The episode ends with genuine laughter, warm hugs, and unanimous agreement: the sandwiches are delicious.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Two-time Tournament of Champions winner and Chopped veteran Maneet Chauhan arrives at the W South Beach as a true Queen of Flavor, demonstrating her Indian Spiced Ceviche, a dish that applies Indian culinary logic to coastal Latin technique. Fresh fish is cured in lime juice in the traditional ceviche manner, then transformed with coconut milk to soften the heat and balance the citrus, and finished with a tarka: the Indian technique of blooming whole spices in searing hot coconut oil and pouring it directly over the dish at the last second. The result is layered, bright, and unexpected. Maneet explains that she grew up in a small town in India and learned to cook by exploring her neighbors' kitchens, each representing a different regional tradition. She recounts a pivotal story from Peru, where she made potato pancakes alongside a local woman without sharing a single word of common languageóconnected entirely through food and gesture. That philosophy runs through her Nashville restaurant, which introduces diners to bold Indian flavors through fusion dishes like hot chicken korma and chicken tikka salad poutine. She draws a parallel between the tarka and the French beurre blancóboth finishing techniques that transform a dish in the final seconds of cooking. Michael, Clinton, and Carla taste the ceviche with genuine surprise and awe.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: As the Chewed Up crew wraps up their week at the W South Beach, Miami-born chef Mika Leon brings the most authentically local dish of the entire trip: Pan Con Lechon, the slow-roasted Cuban pork sandwich that anchors Noche Buena, Cuban Christmas Eve. Mika woke up at 4 AM to start the pork shoulderómarinated overnight in a mojo sauce built on sour orange juice, lime juice, orange juice, and 50 cloves of garlic from her grandfather's recipe. After six hours at 350 degrees, the pork is shredded and crisped with additional mojo in a hot pan, then piled high on a special Cuban rollósweeter and fattier than regular bread, made with lard throughout. Mika shares her journey from dabbling in TV production (she quit after a week) to opening a food truck selling $5 Pan Con Lechons at 3 AM to Miami club-goers, which grew into a full restaurant and now a cookbook. Carla notes that the best cooks often learn from family before any formal training, and this dish proves it. The group discusses Miami's cultural identityómore its own country than a Florida cityódefined by Spanglish, its own traffic logic, and immigrant food traditions passed down for generations. Clinton finally admits that despite a full week at the W, none of them ever made it into the pool.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall makes a vibrant frozen pea soup in under 20 minutes—sautéed onions, stock, mint, parsley, white pepper, and cream blended silky smooth—then leads the hosts in Peep Roulette, a blind flavor-guessing game. Clinton wins; Michael is traumatized.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this “Munch Madness” episode of Chewed Up, Carla Hall, Clinton Kelly, and Michael Symon put eight frozen pizzas through a bracket-style taste test inspired by March Madness. The hosts compare each pairing, breaking down crust, sauce, cheese, and overall flavor while mixing in humor, nostalgia, and brutally honest reactions. As they move through the rounds, the conversation is fueled by strong opinions, unexpected preferences, and plenty of debate about what actually makes a good pizza. Michael’s love of authentic pizza drives passionate critiques, Clinton reflects on his long hiatus from frozen options, and Carla keeps things grounded in everyday practicality for busy households. The competition builds toward a final showdown that sparks lively discussion and a few surprising takes. The episode then shifts into a conversation about potlucks, outdoor dining, and picnics, blending personal preferences with playful arguments. It wraps with a fan giveaway and the hosts’ signature banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly welcomes Stacy London for a fun, nostalgic episode focused on creative ways to use leftover hard-boiled eggs. Stacy makes her signature deviled eggs with a creamy filling and bold toppings like chorizo and jalapeño, while Clinton shares a simple asparagus mimosa finished with grated eggs and vinaigrette. Along the way, the hosts swap stories, talk fashion, and play games, bringing humor and energy to an episode that blends easy recipes with playful conversation.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Hometown Week brings Michael Symon's roots to the Chewed Up kitchen, as he makes his grandfather Pap's potato and farmer's cheese pierogis—a dish that traces back to the heavy Eastern European influence in both Cleveland, where Michael was born, and Pittsburgh, where his parents grew up. The dough is Pap's recipe: all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, one egg, sour cream for tenderness, and softened butter worked in for extra fat and pliability—a forgiving dough that rests in the fridge for an hour before being rolled and cut into rounds. The filling is pure simplicity: Idaho potato riced and folded with farmer's cheese in a 2:1 ratio, seasoned with chives, salt, and pepper. Michael boils the pierogis until they float, then finishes them in a pan with caramelized onions, butter, a splash of pasta water, and fresh chives—garnished tableside with sour cream. The episode's centerpiece story is the birth of Lola's famous beef cheek pierogi: when it first went on the Cleveland menu in 1997, diners balked at 'beef cheek,' and Michael sold only one or two a night. He renamed it 'pot roast pierogi,' sold 70 the next night, ran it for a year until diners were hooked, then quietly switched the name back to beef cheek. It became the restaurant's most popular appetizer by a factor of ten. Carla mentions that her nephew AJ is addicted to Michael's GoldBelly pierogis—available in potato and cheese, pork and sauerkraut, and beef cheek. The episode features three debate topics: leftovers vs. the original meal (unanimous chew it, with the exception of reheated steak and fish); the simplest dishes being the hardest to execute (cacio e pepe, French omelet, pie crust, biscuits, vanilla ice cream); and breakfast for dinner (universal love, best kept occasional—or as a late-night eggs tradition after a couple of old fashioneds). The game is Michael's Two Truths and a Lie: he slipped on butter and dropped a full tray at Lola (true); he set off a fire alarm while cooking at a friend's house (true); and he once cooked pierogis on a George Foreman Grill and melted the plastic drip tray (the lie—Clinton guesses correctly, consistent with Michael's famous contempt for George Foremans, air fryers, and microwaves). Fan mail covers: favorite cake (Clinton: carrot cake with raisins; Michael: coconut; Carla: German chocolate); what to do with canned crab (Michael: fold it into a pierogi filling or make angry crab pasta; Carla: red sauce pasta with crab); and why chefs hate tilapia (muddy flavor, overcrowded farming, general bad juju). The fan giveaway offers a monogrammed Michael Symon apron from his Frontgate collection and a copy of Simply Symon Suppers—won by Sue from Columbus, Georgia. Sponsored by FreshDirect and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes center stage for “Home Week” as he shares a deeply personal dish from his Long Island upbringing: spaghetti with white clam sauce. Drawing on childhood memories of digging for clams in the mudflats of Port Jefferson Station, Clinton walks through a simple yet flavorful recipe using fresh little neck clams, garlic-infused olive oil, white wine, and parsley. As he cooks, the hosts—Carla Hall and Michael Symon—swap stories about growing up, working in restaurants, and stepping outside their comfort zones, with Clinton reflecting on his journey from introversion to becoming a television personality. The episode balances cooking with lively debate, including a spirited discussion about oversized restaurant menus and the etiquette of bringing your own hot sauce to a meal. Michael delivers a passionate rant about seasoning, while Carla offers thoughtful insights on culinary authenticity. The trio’s chemistry shines through playful teasing and shared appreciation for food that tells a story. As the pasta comes together, Clinton emphasizes technique—like gently infusing garlic at low heat and using reserved clam juice for depth of flavor—resulting in a dish that is both accessible and impressive. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting the final product, praising its balance and simplicity. A fun game segment, “Clinton or Clam,” adds humor, followed by fan mail and a giveaway announcement featuring a signed cookbook. The show closes on a warm, communal note, celebrating food, friendship, and the stories behind every dish.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Some call this the ultimate dinner, a perfectly cooked NY steak with a Vodka martini sauce! Chef Stellino once again shares all of his secrets in the making a an iconic sauce , the perfect accompaniment to the king of steaks: The New York. Then to sum it all up he shares with us a lusciously sweet, easy to make dessert of pears braised with Marsala!

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: A soup can be a form of art! Chef Stellino shares his secrets for the perfect soups, smooth , luscious and elegant. Plus he shows the viewers a series of simple techniques to create elegant topping to dress it all up. Simple clear instructions with step by step demonstrations on how to get it done in one try!

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: A great dish does not have to be difficult to make in order to taste great! Fresh ingredients and simple techniques are at the core of these fabulously, delicious recipes. Chef Stellino guides the viewers along each step with humor and passion. He also shares his plating techniques that will help the viewers enhance the look of their finished dishes.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Chef Stellino reconnects with his Italian roots as he guides the viewer through some simple and exciting techniques on how to make traditional Italian pasta dishes with the elegance and panache of an Italian born chef, with a plateful of tips, techniques and ideas to help anyone improve their style and performance in the kitchen.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: The key to every fabulous entrée, is a fabulous sauce. Chef Stellino guides you through a the simple preparation of an elegant sauce for your everyday meal. A blast from the past ,a sweet upside-down pineapple cake that will rekindle your memory and seduce your palate.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Easy does it!, a delicious way to prepare a simple dish packed with flavor and panache, with easy to follow step by step instructions and plating directions. An old school recipe made simple for today's busy home chefs.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Nick cooks pasta amatriciana, clams with sausage and fettunta and the ultimate bread pudding with two chocolates.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores medieval English taverns, inns, and alehouses, each serving different roles and social classes. Taverns sold wine, inns provided lodging, and alehouses were private homes selling ale. The episode covers wine quality control, bread and ale laws, and their reputations for gambling and excess. He recreates bokenade, a beef pottage with saffron, cloves, and verjuice.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores the Old West saloon as a social hub for food and drink. He covers its evolving design, popular beverages like whiskey, beer, champagne, and cocktails, gambling games such as poker, and the famous “free lunch” tradition. The episode highlights regional foods, oysters’ popularity, and recreates an 1886 pork and beans recipe from *Cooking for Profit*.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores WWII home front rationing in the U.S., from 1942 sugar limits to the red and blue point system for meat, dairy, and canned goods. He covers price controls, Victory Gardens, labor shortages, internment camps, and black-market “meatleggers.” He also recreates Betty Crocker’s “emergency steak,” a budget-friendly mix of ground beef, cereal, and milk.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores Titanic’s luxurious first class, from modest suites to lavish parlor suites with private promenades. He covers dining in the saloon and Ritz, daily meals, amenities like the gym and Turkish bath, and the 10+ course final dinner. Using survivor accounts, he recreates the famous peaches in Chartreuse jelly dessert from April 14, 1912.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores Titanic’s first class breakfast from April 11, 1912, featuring over a dozen dishes by Escoffier. He recreates baked apples, smoked salmon coronets, shirred eggs, and buckwheat cakes with blackcurrant conserve. The episode highlights dining venues, passenger pets, and the tragic story of honeymooners Victor and Maria Josefa Peñasco.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max explores the 4–6 month, 2,200-mile Oregon Trail journey (1846–1869), covering provisions like flour, bacon, and hardtack, cooking over buffalo chips, and staple meals like Johnny cakes. He discusses hunting, trading with Native Americans, celebrations, and hardships, including spoiled food, salt-rising bread, pies, and pioneers leaving possessions at Fort Laramie.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max recreates Ötzi the Iceman’s last meal (c. 3,359–3,105 BC), discovered in 1991. He covers Ötzi’s clothing, tools, health (tattoos, heart disease, parasites), and likely murder by arrow. Using stomach contents—smoked ibex, charred red deer, and einkorn pancake with fiddle ferns—Max cooks over an open fire to mimic Neolithic methods.

programme.title: Tasting History with Max Miller
programme.desc: Max traces airline food from 1919’s first commercial meal to aviation’s Golden Age (1950s–70s). He covers flight kitchens, cold-to-hot meal evolution, themed flights, flight attendant hiring, the 1958 Great Sandwich War, and 1978 deregulation. Max recreates a 1954 United Airlines meal of pot roast, vegetables, Delmonico potatoes, and marshmallow-apple salad.

programme.title: Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic Collection 1
programme.desc: Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic is the go-to monkey mechanic for every animal's vehicular needs!

programme.title: Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic Collection 2
programme.desc: Join Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic, as he repairs a whole new bunch of vehicles for even more animal friends!

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: I Like To Ride My Bicycle (Mr. Monkey) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Let's go on a bike ride with Mr. Monkey and friends as we sing along to this Mr. Monkey version of the original Super Simple Song, "I Like To Ride My Bicycle." This is a great song to introduce during transportation themes! I like to ride my bicycle. I ride it every day. I ride it when I go to school. Or when I want to play.

programme.title: What's Your Name? - Kids Song - Finny the Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny the Shark is saying "Hello" and asking the question "What's your name?" This is a fun song to start the day at preschool! Hello, hello. What’s your name? Hello, hello. What’s your name? My name is Finny! My name is Myrtle! Nice to meet you.

programme.title: Wind The Bobbin Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Wind the bobbin up. Wind the bobbin up. Pull, pull, clap, clap, clap.” Get up and dance with this super fun version of the traditional English nursery rhyme.

programme.title: Hot Cross Buns - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s one of our favorite traditional nursery rhymes about food, made super simple. Come sing along with the Chef from our “Do You Know The Muffin Man?” and “Pat-A-Cake” videos. Yum yum!

programme.title: Flying Dragon Fruit Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Come forth brave warriors and watch the Bumble Nums catch some Flying Dragon Fruits for their fiery Flying Dragon Fruit Cake! But be careful! They breathe fire and are very fast!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jerome thinks Jett’s job is easy so he takes his place. He delivers a bathtub to Nayan in Thailand. Boonying the elephant takes a bath but is startled by a spray of water and runs off. Jett arrives to help bring Boonying back.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett carries tires to Alex and his sister Adriana in Monaco. They’ve built a hot dog car for Alex to ride in a soapbox derby. When ducks cause him to run off the course and into town, Paul comes and leads him back with a police escort.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a battery to Noah in Denmark, who’s built a dinosaur out of plastic blocks. When they power it up the dinosaur escapes and runs through the town. Donnie comes and builds another robot to confront the dinosaur and remove his battery.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a microphone to Ruby, a girl in Australia who hosts her own nature program. She interviews animals until a kangaroo snatches her microphone. Bello comes and plays leapfrog with the kangaroo and Ruby gets it all on tape.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a magician’s hat to Moritz in Austria, who is performing an act with his pet rabbit. As a finale, he will make a piano disappear. The naughty rabbit causes the piano to roll out of the theater. Dizzy comes and uses Rescue Ropes to keep the piano from falling into the Danube River and returns it to the theater for the final trick.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a fireman’s hat to Stefan in Germany. His dad is a fireman and is coming to school to tell everyone about his job. But a fire breaks out and the fire truck runs out of water. Donnie uses a fountain as a giant shower head, attaching it to a hose and tapping into underground springs to bring enough water to save the day.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Meena is going to perform a Bollywood dance with her friends. Jett brings the costumes, but there is a mistake and they are all white instead of many colors. The kids dye the outfits but while they are drying the wind blows them away. Jerome arrives to help Jett gather up the costumes and get them back in time for the performance.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett goes to Peru and lands at a small airport where Pilar and her friends greet him. He gives them pins for their Fantastic Flyers Club. They want Jett to take them on an airplane ride but he is not a passenger plane. Jimbo sends Big Wing, who takes them on their very first trip into the sky!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett goes to Bolivia and brings a miner’s helmet to Angelo. His dog has run away. They go with his grandpa to the mine where they take a wild ride on a mining cart, which breaks while underground. Donnie arrives and drills his way through the stone surface to save them.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes a bicycle to Nadia in Bangladesh, who lives on a houseboat with her Mom. Jett teaches her how to ride. When she wants to ride home they run into rain and the road is flooded. Donnie comes and adapts the bike to ride on water. When Nadia arrives home the houseboat is going down the river and the Super Wings must bring it back.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett arrives in rainy London and brings a teddy bear to Cecily. They give the bear an umbrella and take him out to play. The wind lifts the bear away and he flies over the city and falls into a crowd of people with umbrellas. Jerome comes to lead the crowd in a dance number that ends with the bear being revealed and reunited with Cecily.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes a guitar to Shanna in Jamaica, who plays in her family reggae band. They are invited to perform on a ship but miss the boat. Mira comes and transforms a barge into a cool floating stage.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett arrive with a “magic lamp” for Asya in Turkey. She wants a genie to grant her wishes but the lamp does not work, so Jett offers to become her genie. Dizzy comes and helps to fly a magic carpet to Cappadocia where Asya brings ice cream to her Dad who is riding in a hot air balloon.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dema have to find a yeti, but there's a panda cub in danger. How will they save him?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Yaari drives Tippy across the sea, but Tippy gets stuck between two icebergs. How will Jett and his super wing friends save the day?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Mayta and Jett are ready to draw something big in the ground, but it takes forever. What will Jett and Mayta do?

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover sign language! Enjoy upbeat, classical melodies as you learn how to sign words and activities related to common main street businesses, where your food comes from, activities related to dining out and more. Learn through real-life footage, narration, and repetition.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Enjoy the best of summer, autumn and winter with Baby Einstein! Discover the familiar sights and sounds of the different seasons. Learn names of common objects associated with each through repetition and narration and see engaging images of whimsical toys that celebrate each. Discover the magic of each season from falling leaves, rolling beach balls and even snowmen.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Hop aboard to see vehicles that travel by land, water and sky! See real-life images of cars, boats and planes in this on-the-go adventure that features cameos from some of your favorite Baby Einstein characters, all while listening to music from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the wonderful world of water and deep dive into all the amazing creatures that call the ocean home. Learn simple words about the sea like water, coral reef, and ocean through narration and repetition. Engaging live-action footage of your favorite ocean friends and classical melodies will dazzle your little explorer as they experience the magical world under the sea.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating animals from across the globe! Dance and sing along with puppets and discover some of the world's most unique creatures. Join us to dance along to classical melodies as we learn animal names through narration and repetition or simply enjoy seeing live-action footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Every stage is an exciting adventure with your newborn. As babies grow, they absorb a wide variety of sights and sounds. And with so much to see and hear, infants seem to enjoy, and be most soothed by, gentle sounds such as your voice or pleasant, restful music. Treat your child -- and yourself -- to the delights of Baby Bach Musical Adventure, an award-winning program that exposes babies to the joy and majesty of classical music while presenting them with stimulating, colorful images.

programme.title: Farmees Movie Compilation
programme.desc: Farmees brings you the best of nursery rhymes and kids songs. Your kids are sure to enjoy!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: The kids are so excited to learn about dinosaurs. Here is a collection of our best videos.

programme.title: Top 50 Nursery Rhymes Collection - Kids TV
programme.desc: Enjoy the Top 50 nursery rhymes by Kids TV. Babies will learn preschool songs with their favorite characters.

programme.title: Humpty Dumpty Nursery Rhymes and Kids Songs - Farmees
programme.desc: Enjoy Farmees Humpty Dumpty and popular easy to learn nursery rhymes for babies and toddlers, which the children love to sing and dance along with. Learn these kids songs and new words at the same time.

programme.title: Luke and Lily Baby Shark - Shark Song For Kids
programme.desc: Kids! We've taken your favorite songs like Baby Shark, The Muffin Man, Little Bo Peep and put them in a compilation for several minutes of pure, uninterrupted fun learning and singing with Luke and Lily.

programme.title: Daddy's Red Car + Fun Nursery Rhymes for Kindergarten
programme.desc: Kids songs and nursery rhymes that your little one will love! Come join Super Supremes as they sing and dance in this fun kids video. Learn preschool songs, numbers, abc, shapes, colors and more made easy and fun with this nursery rhyme and kids song playlist with rhymes like Daddy’s red car, I spy wheels on the bus, Twinkle Twinkle Little Star and more.

programme.title: Humpty Dumpy & More Kids Rhymes (Little Treehouse)
programme.desc: Do you kids know what happened to Humpty Dumpty when he sat on the wall? Learn this classic nursery rhyme with the Little Tree House gang, plus many other fun songs and dances.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sleet and Dingo plan to collect the taxes owed to Robotnik from the Duke of Velcronia but keep it for themselves.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The sinister Robotnik blackmails Cyrus, an old friend of Sonic's, by holding his father hostage.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Rumor has it that Queen Aleena's journals are buried in the city of Tashistan. Sonic and his siblings search the city for the scrolls.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: While lost in the wastelands of Mobius, Sonic, Manic and Sonia stumble upon an oasis called Lake Valley Resort.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia is invited to a debutante ball, but before she can be officially announced to society, she and her brothers, Manic and Sonic have an important mission.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic joins up with a group of Dr. Robotnik's rejects who have been banished to the dump and helps them understand that their shortcomings were really their strenghs.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Mobius is suffering a crime spree and all the evidence points to Sonic as the culprit. But the real thief is a lab rat wearing a high-tech vehicle suit who was sent by Robotnik to discredit our hero.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When Hacker, an adolescent mole computer whiz steals a super-intelligence program from Robotnik's computer, Sonic has to protect him, smashing Grounder to pieces.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic leaves Tails in charge of protecting a squirrel village food supply, when he zips off to stop a dam from breaking. Robotnik sends Scratch and Grounder after Tails.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik installs a new "obedience microchip" in Scratch and Grounder and orders them to drill for a rare mineral in a tropical mountain. With his Freezomatic machine, Robotnik covers the mountain in ice and runs a Hermit out of his home.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Professor Von Schlemmer (from Too Tall Tails) has created a machine which brings dreams to life. When Robotnik hears about it, he forces the Professor to alter the machine so that nightmares come to life.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik invents a Brain Evolution Achievement Negatizer, or B.E.A.N., in order to make all of Mobius stupid. But instead of turning Sonic and Tails into dummies, the BEAN device turns them into three year old babies!

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik plans to block out the sun and extinguish all life and nature on the planet Mobius. Then he creates a robot son and tries to train him in the ways of wickedness.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik invents an electron altering generator which makes Scratch and Grounder twice as fast as Sonic. When they lose it and Tails picks it up, Tails speeds through the Mobius countryside leaving disasters and accidents in his wake.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: In Part II, Sonic and Sally steal the Roboti-sizer plans, stop the Destroyer from polluting the Great Forest and save Little Sonic, Little Sally and Dulcy's mother, Sabina.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After Dulcy exhibits strange behavior, Sally discovers she's going through a "rites of passage" stage of her adolescence. In Dragon tradition, she is drawn to Dragon Lair, a migratory nesting ground, through trance-like visits from her mother.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell, Ellie, and Kiki are looking hard for something. It turns out that when Thunderbell was trying on Kiki's crown, he accidentally dropped it and a gem went missing. Kiki sadly says that a princess' crown is worthless without a jewel. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that this year they won't be able to enjoy the traditional rainbow dumplings as the recipe was accidentally torn to pieces. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a chef.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables; his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Lemon is ecstatic. She races out of her salon and goes around to the other girls' houses proclaiming that she "did it!" and beckoning them to join her. They follow her back and are completely dazzled by her newest creation - Glamicures. They are brightly colored, jeweled manicures with tiny microchips inside that play songs when the wearer moves her hand. Each girl lines up to receive her new glamicure and soon there is a cacophony of color and sound.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While enjoying lunch in the café, the girls are interrupted by some music coming from the gazebo. They head outside and find Sadiebug and Kadiebug wearing badges and standing by a banner and podium. A group of chairs are set up in front of them. The twins begin to announce, and then argue, that they are starting a club - a good citizens club. The girls are shocked as the twins argue with each other all the time.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With all the Baby Berrykins running around Berry Bitty City, Princess Berrykin decides to build a Day Care Center and playground. But she's worried because it's dull-looking - all grey metal and brown wood. She asks Strawberry whom might be good to help her out. And two names immediately come to her mind - Lemon and Raspberry! The Princess confesses she's always admired their sense of design and style.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: After returning to the café after a walk with her pets, Strawberry is met by Mr. Longface. He's been waiting for her so that they can get started making treats and decorating for Sadiebug's and Kadiebug's party that afternoon. But Custard and Pupcake have enjoyed their time outside so much that they want to continue playing.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables; his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest; she wants to fit in and contribute to the community.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Raspberry is discovered by a talent scout who wants her to move to Berry Big City and design a new line of Glitter fashions! She will be provided with her own studio, staff, publicity, commitments from big department stores-everything she needs to be a big fashion designing star! All her friends are thrilled for her. Only Cherry is a bit less enthusiastic about Raspberry's stardom, which Raspberry mistakenly interprets as jealousy.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With the summer almost over, Blueberry overhears Strawberry talking about a wonderful tropical island and believes her friend is going to surprise them all with a much needed vacation! Blueberry makes the other girls promise to keep the secret until Strawberry reveals the big surprise. Of course, even though they've promised to keep the secret, they can't help but try and suss out what the island's like from every little thing Strawberry does.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry and Orange are dangling their legs in the stream when something tickles Orange's foot. She is sent squealing from the water, certain an eel is after her. Strawberry good-naturedly chuckles at her antics. But Orange gets her dander up and wades into the water to face her "attacker." When she looks down into the water, gazing up at her is the cutest, most adorable, puffy-cheeked fish she's ever seen. It's love at first sight. Orange has to take him home.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While at Orange's general store to pick up a package, Berrykin Bloom chats with Orange about the pogostick he gave her to help her reach the upper shelves. She is very appreciative. He ends up getting a package of special berry seeds he ordered, not the vacation magazine Orange thought he came for. He explains how the seeds were a better purchase because of all the benefits to the Berryworks but now he can no longer afford a vacation.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother and Sister forget their manners. Then, Mama organizes Papa’s workshop.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Sister adopts a chipmunk. Mama teaches Sister the 'Star light, star bright' principle.

programme.desc: The Bear Family gets new neighbors. Then, Papa Bear decides to run for Mayor himself!

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Sister enters the dog in a pet show. Brother builds a birdhouse instead of tidying.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: The Bear Family find mementos in the attic. The cubs learn their friends are moving away.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: The Bears host a family reunion. Brother and Sister accidentally let cows loose!

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother and Sister adopt a puppy. Brother and Sister damage Ms. Grizzle’s flowerbed.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Papa wants to remove an old tree stump. Then, Brother joins an art class.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother tutors Sister. Papa’s friendship with Farmer Ben is tested.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Papa takes the cubs fishing. Then, Brother gets his own key to the tree house.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother helps with the school talent show. The Bear Family vacations in an old lighthouse.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama and Papa let Sister go to a sleep over. Brother complains he has too much homework.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Sister does an assignment on Brother. Sister is jealous of Brother's three-speed racer.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama and Papa do a dance demonstration. Brother and Sister love the Space Grizzlies show.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother and Sister must clean their room. Then, Papa Bear gets a case of the hiccups!

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama takes the cubs outside. Then, Sister gets a haircut.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After the kids discuss their desire for a special day, Beth creates Happy Day, a special holiday in which each friend makes a wish and the others try to make it come true. When Beth hurts herself, BJ learns the importance of doing good deeds for others.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney plans a magical picnic for his friends and their families to spend quality time together and create lasting memories. Each kid has brought a family treasure to show Barney, which they all excitedly spend time sharing with one another.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The kids gather at the park, talking about how hot it is and wondering what to do on such a day. Thanks to Barney's brilliant idea, what starts as a too hot afternoon turns into a totally cool day as he introduces them to the wonders of water. Excitedly, they all team up to find creative ways to stay cool and have a splashing good time together!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Even though the park feels like a second home for Barney and his friends, BJ dreams of building his very own totally awesome, completely cool house. While they eagerly wait to see it, they start talking about the different rooms a house can have. However, when BJ forgets to include a space for Baby Bop in his plans, Barney reminds him that family is what truly makes a house feel like a home.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Mr. Boyd excitedly tells Barney and the kids about the upcoming arrival of a brand-new fountain for the park! While they eagerly await the special delivery, Barney and the children make the most of their time by enjoying the park's delights. They swing on the swings, admire the beautiful flowers, and create wonderful memories together, all while looking forward to the fountain's grand entrance.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The children gather for an exciting game of on and off, which turns into a learning adventure for Barney, Baby Bop, and their friends. They explore all the different ways things can be turned on and off, discovering new ideas along the way. Later, Barney and his friends join forces to help Baby Bop find adorable costumes for her beloved teddy bear, making it a delightful day full of play and discovery!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: In the park, Mr. Boyd is organizing a special toy drive to help children in need. When Baby Bop feels unsure about donating her toys, Barney and his friends come together to show her the joy of sharing and how it can make a difference, particularly in someone else's life.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Whitney introduces Angela to the concept of an animal shelter as she shares an adorable picture of kittens. When Miss Jo misplaces her beloved cat, Cody, she decides to place a lost cat sign in the park to notify anyone who passes by. Barney and the children eagerly join the search. Throughout the search, the kids learn all about cats and the responsibilities of pet care.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Gianna and Kami have a delightful dress-up session, followed by a charming tea party with Barney as their special guest. Meanwhile, Mario announces hed like to write his own fairy tale. In their eagerness to help Mario, the children enthusiastically act out some of their most cherished fairy tales, offering him ideas and inspiration.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The children discuss the various ways to cool off on a hot day, especially with a nice cold drink of water or some ice cream. Throughout the day, Barney and the children learn about hot and cold, but the fun really begins when Barney turns the playground into a winter/summer wonderland!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When Sarah accidentally spills red paint into the blue paint, a mess turns into a perfectly purple day. The kids use this opportunity to explore the creation of different colors by mixing them together. Inspired by this delightful turn of events, Barney and the kids organize a surprise party for Colleen, entirely centered around her most beloved color: purple.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Tony has a box filled with numbers that he made with his dad and is proudly showing them to everyone. Suddenly, a strong gust of wind scatters the numbers all around! Barney and his friends team up to go on an exciting number hunt, helping Tony find and collect his homemade numbers again.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Angela is in the park to release her turtle, Terry, after observing him with her classmates in school. Barney, Nick, and Sarah are delighted to see Terry come out of his shell, which leads to a discussion about the meanings of in and out. Baby Bop arrives with her chrysalis, and together with Angela's turtle, Barney and his friends are inspired to do In and Out activities.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Its a rainy spring day, and the children can be found inside painting and creating. The weather becomes an exciting opportunity for the kids to learn all about the season of Spring and discuss all the fun activities they can partake in this season. This includes flying a kite, riding a bike, and enjoying the rainbows in the sky.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After feeling tired of being told to be careful, BJ learns that saying be careful is a way of showing love and concern. This important lesson prompts Barney to teach his friends essential safety rules. Spending time with Barney is always enjoyable, but it's also crucial to learn how to play it safe to stay protected and happy!
